content: User replied to a review thanking a 43-year-old customer who found Shadow PC easy to use for running Steam games on Oculus despite not being computer-savvy.
evidence_span: Reply to this review and thank them: I'm 43 and not the smartest when it comes to computers and such. I wanted to run steam games on my oculus but couldn't afford a computer this service solved my issue and was relatively easy to use and access.
assistant_replies: Thank you so much for sharing your experience with Shadow PC! We're glad to hear that our service was able to provide a solution for your gaming needs and that it was relatively easy for you to use and access, despite not being the most tech-savvy. We value customer feedback and are constantly working to improve and make our service more user-friendly. We're glad we could make it possible for you to run steam games on your Oculus and we hope you will continue to have a great experience with our service. Thank you for choosing Shadow PC!

content: User plans to use as much open source AI components and models as possible to save on development time and engineers.

dimension.reason: to save on development time and engineers

relation.subject: User
relation.object: open source AI components and models

dimension.state_value: use as much open source AI components and models as possible

evidence_span: We will use as much open source AI components and models as possible to save on the development time and engineers
assistant_replies:
Using open source AI components and models can be a cost-effective way to build an AI-based toolset, as it can save you time and resources compared to building everything from scratch. When using open source AI components and models, you will need to consider the following resources:

1. Development resources: You will need a team of developers with the technical skills and expertise needed to integrate the open source AI components and models into your toolset. This may include machine learning engineers, data scientists, and software developers with expertise in building AI-based systems.
2. Data and training resources: You will need a large dataset of content to train and validate your AI system. This may include abstract shapes, alphabet characters, real-world objects, music, and other audio content. You may need to create this data yourself, or purchase it from a third party.
3. Hardware resources: Depending on the complexity of your AI system, you may need to allocate resources for hardware such as servers, GPUs, or other specialized hardware.
4. Testing and quality assurance: You will need to allocate resources to test and validate the AI-based toolset to ensure it meets your quality standards and performs as expected. This may include a testing team or an individual with expertise in AI testing.
5. Marketing and sales: You will need to consider the resources needed to reach and sell to your target customers, including any marketing or sales materials that will be required. This may include a marketing team or individual with expertise in AI-based tools marketing.
6. Customer support: Depending on the complexity of your AI-based toolset, you may need to allocate resources to provide customer support and troubleshooting assistance to users. This may include a customer support team or individual with expertise in AI-based tools support.

I hope this information is helpful! Let me know if you have any other questions about the resources needed to create an AI-based toolset using open source components and models.

content: User notes that ready-to-use text-to-video AI models generate poor realistic video but are brilliant for generating simple things needed.

relation.subject: text-to-video AI models
relation.object: brilliant for generating simple things needed

evidence_span: There are ready-to-be-used text-to-video AI models. They generate poor video in terms of realistically looking things, but they are absolutely brilliant when generating simple things like we need.
assistant_replies:
Text-to-video AI models can be useful for generating simple video content such as abstract shapes, alphabet characters, and other basic graphics. These models are typically not as advanced as more complex models that are designed to generate more realistic video content, but they can be a cost-effective way to generate basic video content quickly and efficiently.

To use a text-to-video AI model to generate content for your mobile app, you will need to consider the following resources:

1. AI model: You will need to select an appropriate text-to-video AI model for your needs. There are several commercial and open source models available, so you will need to research and compare options to find the best fit for your requirements.
2. Development resources: You will need a team of developers with the technical skills and expertise needed to integrate the text-to-video AI model into your toolset. This may include machine learning engineers, data scientists, and software developers with expertise in building AI-based systems.
3. Data and training resources: You may need to provide data to the text-to-video AI model in order to train it or fine-tune its output. This may include text descriptions of the video content you want to generate, as well as any other relevant data.
4. Hardware resources: Depending on the complexity of your AI system, you may need to allocate resources for hardware such as servers, GPUs, or other specialized hardware.
5. Testing and quality assurance: You will need to allocate resources to test and validate the AI-based toolset to ensure it meets your quality standards and performs as expected. This may include a testing team or an individual with expertise in AI testing.
6. Marketing and sales: You will need to consider the resources needed to reach and sell to your target customers, including any marketing or sales materials that will be required. This may include a marketing team or individual with expertise in AI-based tools marketing.
7. Customer support: Depending on the complexity of your AI-based toolset, you may need to allocate resources to provide customer support and troubleshooting assistance to users. This may include a customer support team or individual with expertise in AI-based tools support.

I hope this information is helpful! Let me know if you have any other questions about the resources needed to create an AI-based toolset using a text-to-video model.

content: User states they will need one UI/UX designer, one mobile app developer, and one AI engineer.

relation.subject: User
relation.object: one UI/UX designer, one mobile app developer, one AI engineer

dimension.state_value: UI/UX designer, mobile app developer, AI engineer

evidence_span: So, we will need one UI/UX designer, one mobile app developer, one AI engineer.
assistant_replies:
To create a mobile app that integrates with an AI-based toolset for content creation, you will likely need the following resources:

1. UI/UX designer: A UI/UX designer will be responsible for creating the user interface and user experience of the mobile app. They will work with the development team to design the layout and flow of the app, as well as the look and feel of the user interface.
2. Mobile app developer: A mobile app developer will be responsible for building the mobile app and integrating it with the AI-based toolset. They will need expertise in the platform(s) you are targeting (e.g. iOS, Android), as well as experience in integrating mobile apps with AI-based systems.
3. AI engineer: An AI engineer will be responsible for building and maintaining the AI-based toolset. They will need expertise in machine learning, data science, and AI-based systems development.

Depending on the complexity of your project, you may also need additional resources such as testers, marketing and sales staff, and customer support staff.

I hope this information is helpful! Let me know if you have any other questions about the resources needed to create a mobile app that integrates with an AI-based toolset for content creation.

content: User states UX/UI guy will cost about $1K per month.
evidence_span: UX/UI guy will cost us about $1K per month
assistant_replies:
The cost of hiring a UI/UX designer can vary depending on the designer's level of experience, skills, and location. In general, you can expect to pay more for a designer with more experience and specialized skills. In Eastern Europe, the cost of hiring a UI/UX designer may be lower compared to other regions, but this will depend on the specific market and job market in the region.

It is difficult for me to provide a more specific estimate for the cost of hiring a UI/UX designer in Eastern Europe, as there are many variables that can affect the cost. Some factors to consider when determining the budget for a UI/UX designer include:

1. The designer's level of experience: More experienced designers may command higher rates due to their expertise and track record.
2. The complexity of the project: If your project is more complex or requires specialized skills, you may need to pay more for a designer with the necessary expertise.
3. The duration of the project: The cost of hiring a UI/UX designer may be higher if the project is longer in duration, as the designer will be working on the project for an extended period of time.
4. The location of the designer: The cost of living in different regions can affect the rates that designers charge. In general, you may pay more for a designer located in a region with a higher cost of living.

I hope this information is helpful! Let me know if you have any other questions about the cost of hiring a UI/UX designer in Eastern Europe.

content: User asks if they can provide data to extract interesting statistics for a business development manager or sales manager.

relation.subject: User
relation.object: extracting statistics for business development or sales manager

evidence_span: Can I provide you a bunch of data that would could then extract interesting statistics that would be informative for a business development manager or sales manager?
assistant_replies: Yes, you can provide data and I can assist with extracting statistics that may be informative for a business development or sales manager. However, the relevance of the statistics and their potential impact on the business would depend on the specific context and goals of the company.

content: User states the goal is to sell more Terms and Conditions.
evidence_span: The goals are to sell more Terms and Conditions.
assistant_replies:
Understood. To extract relevant statistics for selling more Terms and Conditions, you may need to analyze data such as:

1. Sales trends: understand the current sales volume and patterns of the T&C product.
2. Customer demographics: gather information on the target audience, including age, gender, location, and buying habits.
3. Market analysis: understand the market demand for T&C products and identify potential competition.
4. Customer feedback: gather feedback from previous customers to identify areas of improvement and what they value in a T&C product.

By analyzing this data, you may be able to identify opportunities for growth, target areas for improvement, and develop effective sales strategies to increase T&C sales.

content: User has data on newly registered businesses in specific areas in Auckland, including 6 months of new registrations per sector and registration dates.

relation.subject: User
relation.object: newly registered businesses in Auckland areas

dimension.state_value: 6 months of new registrations per sector and dates in Auckland areas

evidence_span: The data I have is on newly registered businesses in specific areas in Auckland. The demographics of these areas I do not know. But I can provide 6months worth of new registrations in each sector of business. Also the date a of registrations.
assistant_replies:
Understood. With the data you have provided, you could extract the following statistics to gain insights into the potential market for T&C products:

1. Business sector distribution: identify the most common business sectors and the trends in their growth over time.
2. Regional trends: analyze the distribution of business registrations across specific areas in Auckland to identify hotspots of new business activity.
3. Seasonality: examine the seasonality of business registrations to determine if certain times of the year are more favorable for T&C sales.
4. Market saturation: determine if certain sectors or regions have a high concentration of businesses, indicating a potentially saturated market.

By analyzing this data, you may be able to determine which sectors and regions have a high potential for T&C sales and tailor your sales strategy accordingly.

content: User asks if CSV format would work for supplying data.
evidence_span: Yes. But to supply to you, would csv work ok as it’s text based?
assistant_replies: Yes, CSV is a good format to supply the data to me. As a text-based format, CSV files are easy to work with and can be imported into most data analysis tools for further analysis. The structure of a CSV file, with each row representing a single data point and each column representing a specific variable, makes it ideal for storing and analyzing tabular data.

content: User provided the format of the data with columns: nzbn, entityName, changeDateTime, createdAt, classificationDescription, postCodes.

relation.object: nzbn, entityName, changeDateTime, createdAt, classificationDescription, postCodes

The columns and data types are well defined, which makes it easy to understand the structure of the data and perform analysis. The use of a timestamp data type for the "changeDateTime" and "createdAt" columns will allow you to perform time-based analysis on the data, such as tracking the distribution of business registrations over time. The "classificationDescription" column provides information on the business sector, while the "postCodes" column allows you to analyze the distribution of business registrations across different regions.
